PROFILE SUMMARY  
I am a seasoned development professional with over 20 years of progressive experience in program 
development, project management, monitoring and evaluation (M&E/MEAL), research, proposal and 
grants writing, and multi -sectoral program implementation. My exp erience spans education, health, 
disaster response, climate change, infrastructure, livelihoods, and social development, working across 
humanitarian and development contexts in Pakistan.  
 
I hold a Master’s degree in Sociology, a Master’s degree in Economics, a BBA in Management, along 
with additional professional qualifications in Supply Chain Management. I have worked extensively 
with international financial institutions, UN agencies, bila teral donors, and government counterparts, 
including the World Bank, Asian Development Bank (ADB), WHO, FAO, UNDP, UNICEF, and multiple 
federal, provincial, and district government departments across Pakistan.  
 
I am currently serving as General Manager – Program Development & Monitoring and Evaluation 
(M&E) at SAFWCO,  where I provide strategic leadership for program design, institutional fundraising, 
donor engagement, and results -based performance management. In this role, I oversee the 
development of high -quality concept notes and proposals, lead organization -wide MEA L frameworks, 
ensure donor compliance, and support senior management in managing multi -donor portfolios and 
strategic partnerships with UN agencies,  INGOs, and government institutions.  
 
Prior to this, I served as Head of Grants & Partnerships at JDC Welfare Foundation,  where I led 
resource mobilization, donor coordination, and partnership development. I was responsible for 
expanding institutional donor engagement, securing multi -million PKR funding, strengthening 
partnerships with UN agencies, INGOs, government departme nts, and private -sector donors, and 
ensuring compliance with donor regulations and cross -cutting priorities such as gender equality, 
protection, and localization.  
 
Earlier in my career, I worked for more than a decade as Manager – Program Development at the 
Management & Development Centre (MDC).  In this role, I led the design and development of 
research proposals, technical bids, evaluations, and donor -funded projects, contributing significantly 
to transforming MDC from a local organization into a leading national consultancy firm. In the most 
recent year of my tenure alone, I supported the mobilization of over PKR 1 billion in consultancy and 
project funding through competitive donor engagements with the EU, DFID/FCDO, UN agencies, 
World Bank, and ADB.  
 
Across these roles, I have demonstrated strong capabilities in strategic program design, donor 
engagement, consortium coordination, and organizational growth, combined with hands -on 
experience in fragile, disaster -prone, and remote areas. My work is ground ed in strong contextual 
understanding, accountability, and evidence -based decision -making.  
 
Core Competencies  
• Program and project design, implementation, and management  
• Grants writing, research proposal development, and budgeting  
• Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability & Learning (MEAL)  
• Baseline surveys, assessments, evaluations, and third -party validations  
• Logical framework analysis and results -based management  
• Donor coordination, partnership development, and resource mobilization

Position: General Manager – Program Development & Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E)  
Department:  Program Development and Implementation  
Organization:  SAFWCO (Sindh Agricultural and Forestry Workers Coordinating Organization)  
Duty Station:  Pakistan  
Duration:  January, 2026 – Present  
Reporting Line:  Executive Director / Chief Executive Officer  
Supervisory Responsibility:  Program Development, Grants, Partnerships, and M&E/MEAL Teams  
Key Duties & Responsibilities:  
1. Strategic Leadership and Program Development  
• Provide strategic leadership for organizational program development, institutional 
fundraising, and results -based performance management across humanitarian and 
development portfolios.  
• Lead the design, development, and submission of multi -sectoral programs, including concept 
notes, proposals, and expressions of interest for UN agencies, bilateral and multilateral 
donors, INGOs, and foundations.  
• Ensure programs are aligned with organizational strategy, donor priorities, humanitarian 
principles, and international development standards.  
2. Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability, and Learning (MEAL/M&E)  
• Oversee the design, implementation, and strengthening of MEAL and M&E systems across all 
programs, ensuring compliance with donor standards and integration of results -based 
management (RBM).  
• Develop and maintain monitoring frameworks, indicators, and tools to track program 
performance, outcomes, and impact.  
• Lead data collection, analysis, and reporting to inform evidence -based decision -making, 
learning, and continuous program improvement.  
• Ensure high -quality, timely, and results -oriented donor reporting, maintaining coherence 
between narrative, financial, and M&E components.  
• Promote the integration of cross -cutting priorities (gender equality, protection, safeguarding, 
climate resilience, inclusion, and localization) into M&E systems and reporting.  
3. Grants, Donor Relations, and Partnerships  
• Manage and strengthen relationships with UN agencies, government counterparts, INGOs, 
consortium partners, and private -sector stakeholders.  
• Represent SAFWCO in donor coordination meetings, technical forums, and partnership 
platforms at national and provincial levels.  
• Ensure compliance with donor contractual obligations, including quality assurance, reporting, 
and performance monitoring.  
4. Cross -Functional Coordination  
• Coordinate closely with program, finance, procurement, and M&E teams to ensure alignment 
of proposals, budgets, work plans, and results frameworks.  
• Support integration of M&E findings into program design, implementation, and organizational 
decision -making.  
5. Team Leadership and Capacity Development  
• Lead and supervise program development, grants, partnerships, and M&E teams, providing 
strategic guidance, performance management, and capacity building.  
• Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, learning, and results -oriented performance.  
6. Organizational Strategy and Sustainability

Position: Director – Grants and Partnerships

Reporting Line:  Executive Director / Chief Executive Officer

Key Duties and Responsibilities  
1. Resource Mobilization and Fundraising Leadership  
• Lead organizational resource mobilization and diversified fundraising strategy, securing 
funding from UN agencies, bilateral and multilateral donors, INGOs, foundations, government 
institutions, corporate partners, and digital fundraising platforms.  
• Strengthen JDC’s global fundraising footprint by leveraging online crowdfunding platforms, 
diaspora engagement, and digital donor communication strategies.  
• Lead and manage international digital fundraising campaigns on platforms such as 
LaunchGood and GlobalGiving, including campaign design, narrative development, donor 
outreach, performance tracking, and reporting.  
2. Grant Acquisition and Compliance Management  
• Oversee the development of high -quality concept notes, grant proposals, expressions of 
interest, and funding submissions, ensuring strategic alignment, technical robustness, and 
compliance with donor guidelines.  
• Ensure effective grant acquisition, contract negotiation, and lifecycle compliance 
management, maintaining adherence to financial, procurement, and reporting standards.  
• Oversee timely and high -quality narrative, financial, and results -based reporting, ensuring 
coherence between technical implementation, budgets, KPIs, and logical frameworks.  
3. Strategic Partnerships and CSR Engagement  
• Develop and manage strategic partnerships across public sector institutions, multilateral 
organizations, humanitarian actors, corporate/CSR partners, and philanthropic foundations.  
• Design and implement engagement strategies for both institutional donors and corporate 
partners, aligning programming with donor priorities, ESG frameworks, and sustainability 
goals.  
• Serve as the focal point for donor and partner relations, including negotiations, agreement 
finalization, compliance oversight, and strategic communication.  
4. Programmatic and Cross -Functional Coordination  
• Coordinate closely with program, finance, procurement, and MEAL teams to ensure alignment 
between proposals, budgets, implementation plans, and results frameworks.  
• Integrate cross -cutting priorities, including gender equality, protection, safeguarding, climate 
resilience, accountability to affected populations, and localization principles.  
5. Team Leadership and Capacity Development  
• Lead and supervise the Grants, Partnerships, and Resource Mobilization Team, providing 
strategic direction, quality assurance, and capacity development.  
• Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and innovation across fundraising and 
partnership teams.  
6. Representation and Advocacy  
• Represent JDC Welfare Foundation in donor coordination forums, corporate partnership 
meetings, CSR platforms, and international fundraising engagements.  
• Contribute to organizational funding pipeline management, sustainability strategy, and 
diversification of revenue streams, strengthening JDC’s financial resilience.
